action.reasoning: Observation: iter 27 alpha tuning confirmed best Ridge alpha=1, mean_r2=0.304 unchanged. MLP from scratch trained earlier (iter 26) gave catastrophic negative R². Analysis: The linear Ridge model captures most linear variance; remaining non-linear signal is small but could improve r2_q (currently 0.135). Starting MLP from the linear solution (initialize hidden layer with Ridge weights, output layer as identity) can provide a warm start, avoiding instability, and fine-tune residuals. Only ~10 min remaining; one epoch fine-tune on full 10.1M samples (≈2 min) is feasible and could boost test score. Decision: write mlp_fine_tune.py to build an MLP initialized from Ridge W/b, train 1 epoch with low LR, clip gradients, evaluate val R² on subsample, save checkpoint and update predict config if improved. Then submit eval immediately.
